purpose: Generic error dialog with red-tinted 'Error' title (rendered as a colored span using --color-failed CSS var). Single Close button. Used to surface caught exceptions or API failures with a consistent themed look rather than alert().
gotchas: Title prop is JSX (a colored span) — Modal passes it to aria-label, which may not be screen-reader-friendly when the value is a React node rather than a plain string.
Only displays a single message string — for multi-line errors, callers must pre-format with newlines and the CSS class .modal-message-error must preserve whitespace (white-space: pre-line).
patterns: Same minimal wrapping pattern as ConfirmModal — Modal + message paragraph + .modal-actions row.
Uses --color-failed CSS variable rather than a hardcoded red — themable across all 6 theme presets.
conventions: Reuses .modal-message and .modal-btn classes shared with ConfirmModal.
